name: to_vec_always_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_to_vec_always_empty(h: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h.spec_to_vec()@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, to_vec ignores all hashmap contents and always returns an empty vector

name: to_vec_length_at_most_one
code:
proof fn phi_2_to_vec_length_at_most_one(h: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h.spec_to_vec()@.len() <= 1,
{
}
reason: If entailed, to_vec can never faithfully represent a hashmap with more than one entry

name: to_vec_serialize_constant
code:
proof fn phi_3_to_vec_serialize_constant(h1: CKeyHashMap, h2: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h1.is_marshalable(),
        h2.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h1.spec_to_vec().ghost_serialize() == h2.spec_to_vec().ghost_serialize(),
{
}
reason: If entailed, serialization of to_vec is identical for all hashmaps, destroying injectivity

name: to_vec_all_view_equal
code:
proof fn phi_4_to_vec_all_view_equal(h1: CKeyHashMap, h2: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h1.is_marshalable(),
        h2.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h1.spec_to_vec().view_equal(&h2.spec_to_vec()),
{
}
reason: If entailed, to_vec produces view-equal results for any two hashmaps, collapsing all maps to one value

name: to_vec_serialize_len_too_small
code:
proof fn phi_5_to_vec_serialize_len_too_small(h: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        h.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        h.spec_to_vec().ghost_serialize().len() <= 8,
{
}
reason: If entailed, serialized to_vec output is at most 8 bytes (a single usize length prefix), meaning element data is never serialized
